_comment_2: This file defines how reservation data is mapped to Google Sheets
_note_language: Language code: 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish. Affects month tab names.

january_reservations._note_tab: Name of the Google Sheets tab (use Spanish months if language=es: Enero, Febrero, etc.)
january_reservations._note_range: Cell where data starts (top-left corner of data table)
january_reservations._note_columns: Column headers in your CSV (defines data structure)

Guest Name._note: Maps CSV 'Guest' field to first column (offset 0)

Check In._note: Maps CSV 'Start Date' to second column (offset 1)

Check Out._note: Maps CSV 'End Date' to third column (offset 2)

Total Price._note: Calculated field: sums CSV 'Price' + 'Commission' into fifth column (offset 4)

january_reservations._note_mapping: Each key matches a column name. Use 'csv_field' for direct mapping or 'csv_fields' + 'operation' for calculations.
january_reservations._note_physical: Total number of columns in this section (must match number of columns defined)

_example_costs_tab._comment: Example of a costs/expenses tab configuration

_example_skip_column._comment: Example showing how to skip a column (leave it blank)

Guest Name._note: skip_next: true skips the next column (leaves it blank)

_example_skip_column._note: Physical columns includes the skipped column

february_reservations._comment: Repeat for each month. Copy january_reservations structure and change tab_name.

_notes.quick_start: 1. Copy this file to {apartment}_{year}.json | 2. Update spreadsheet_id | 3. Configure tabs for each month | 4. Run: rez upload data.csv -a {apartment}
_notes.csv_field_names: Must match exact column names in your CSV exports from Airbnb/Booking.com
_notes.sheet_col_offset: 0-indexed column position relative to start_range (0=first column, 1=second, etc.)
_notes.calculated_fields: Use 'csv_fields' array + 'operation' to combine multiple CSV columns. Currently supports: 'sum'
_notes.skip_next: Use skip_next: true to leave a blank column in the sheet (useful for formula columns)
_notes.physical_columns: Total columns including skipped ones. Used to calculate clearing range.
_notes.all_months: Define all 12 months if you want year-round tracking. Use English (January-December) or Spanish (Enero-Diciembre) based on language setting.
